hypothesis: Systems-optimizer history becomes safer if every kept or rejected backlog card must resolve to a matching hypothesis-card artifact, so replay, reporting, and learning retention do not depend only on the latest terminal state pointers.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` only required replayable hypothesis-card artifacts for `state.last_kept_change` and `state.last_rejected_change`, so 12 kept/rejected backlog items still had no `CLAW/control-plane/system-optimizer/hypotheses/<id>.json` file and the verifier reported clean. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any kept or rejected backlog item that lacks a matching hypothesis card artifact or drifts from it across the bounded contract fields and `learning_item`, while `recorded_at` stays aligned to `learning_log` and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if every resolved systems-optimizer backlog card must retain a matching replayable hypothesis-card artifact and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Resolved systems-optimizer history must keep replayable hypothesis-card artifacts for every kept/rejected card; terminal pointers alone leave older ratchets unverifiable.
